questions.question: What is the primary goal of clustering algorithms?
questions.explanation: Clustering algorithms group similar data points into clusters based on their characteristics.

questions.question: Which of the following proximity measures is not a common distance metric used in clustering?
questions.explanation: Cosine similarity is commonly used in text clustering, but not typically used in traditional clustering algorithms.

questions.question: What is the main difference between hierarchical and non-hierarchical clustering algorithms?
options: A) Hierarchical algorithms build clusters from bottom-up
B) Non-hierarchical algorithms build clusters from top-down
C) Hierarchical algorithms are faster than non-hierarchical algorithms
D) Non-hierarchical algorithms are more scalable
questions.explanation: Hierarchical clustering algorithms build clusters from bottom-up, where each cluster is a subset of the previous one, whereas non-hierarchical algorithms build clusters from top-down.

questions.question: Which clustering algorithm is suitable for identifying clusters of varying densities?
questions.explanation: Hierarchical clustering can identify clusters of varying densities, but DBSCAN is more commonly used for this purpose.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using DBSCAN clustering algorithm?
options: A) It is faster than other clustering algorithms
B) It is more scalable than other clustering algorithms
C) It can handle noise and outliers
D) It can handle high-dimensional data
questions.explanation: DBSCAN is able to handle noise and outliers, making it a robust choice for clustering algorithms.
